titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Wilfrid Spender to Lady Lillian Spender, 27 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Wilfrid Bliss Spender

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Sir Wilfrid Bliss Spender (1876-1960) to his wife, Lady Lillian Spender, née Dean, (1880-1968). In this letter, Spender gives an account of his settling into his new position and his workload. He discusses the local surroundings, the character of the Corps Commander, and his own recent promotion, which he believes will be only temporary.Sir Wilfrid Spender was a member of the British Army until 1913 when controversy surrounding his signature of the Ulster Solemn League and Covenant resulted in his resignation. Spender was called up for military service in 1914 and from 1915 served in the 36th (Ulster) Division. Throughout the war, Spender wrote a collection of letters to his wife Lady Lillian Spender. The letters date mainly from 1915 to 1918, when Sir Wilfrid was serving in France. The letters concern politics, the Irish question, personal life, and Wilfrid's experience of the Great War. Lady Lillian served in the Ulster Volunteer Nursing Corps during the war, and was an organiser of the Ulster Division Comforts Fund.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
July 27 My Pr, For some reason I did not
sleep very well last night & had
to make an very early rise to drive
up to the German lines so am not
in a pos con dition to write much. Gradually I am picking up my
work but it is still indefinite
& of course in a new area am
feels strange tho' it is not quite
as new country as the North
was. No letter for 3 days
a very hungry b w. Last night & this morning
I was up at a Captured German
village which has been very
much in evidence lately, A more
mournful sight one has never
seen debris dust debris dust
Pompeii was not in it with
this. The are relieving function was
no man's land where many young
rather uncommon wildflowers
had taken the chance of man's
absence to start a home. Rather
a mistake I'm afraid as it
will soon be as dusty &
full as the rest of the country. My Corps Commander is Gen
Horn, who has a big reputation
as a strong man, probably
rather silent. I have seen
little of him but have been
moved to the A mess today
so shall see more presumably. I fancy I am more or
less under trial & may find
myself being transferred to
a Division if not found wanting.
I am most anxious not to
supersede the proper GSO 2 who
is an excellent chap - a senior
major - . I may not give
satisfaction here in which case
I shall drop back to Capt.
again, like many others- to
major - Nonewhich I do
my best when people
call out. Colonel not to
show that I think
they are addressing
someone else, & not to jump when
Sentries present arms. Must fly back to
the office. Your b w
WB Spender
